consequence.description: Without a consensus gate, AI signals can trigger trades that exceed position limits or violate volatility constraints, causing losses that would not occur with proper risk oversight

core.action: Assume ML/AI components are properly validated with training/test split or walk-forward validation — the framework does not implement validation controls; AI models may be overfitted to historical patterns

consequence.description: Without proper train/test split or walk-forward validation, ML/AI models memorize historical patterns and appear profitable in backtesting but fail in live trading due to overfitting

core.when: When implementing any ML/AI model for trading signal generation
core.action: Implement temporal train/test split with non-overlapping time windows or walk-forward validation with expanding/sliding windows; evaluate model performance on out-of-sample periods before deployment

consequence.description: Without validation, overfitted models produce unrealistic backtest returns that cannot be achieved in live trading, leading to deployment of models that lose money in production

core.when: When implementing order execution and position tracking logic
core.action: Assume the framework implements order state machine with fill tracking — the framework does not track order execution states; each orders are treated as immediately filled at submission

consequence.description: Without order state tracking, the system assumes immediate fill causing ghost positions where the backtest shows positions that never actually existed in live trading due to execution latency or rejection

evidence_refs.summary: e293bf7 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-034

core.when: When implementing order submission and position management
core.action: Implement an order state machine with states: PENDING, PARTIAL_FILLED, FILLED, REJECTED, CANCELLED; update position only upon FILLED confirmation; record submission_time and fill_time for latency tracking

consequence.description: Without state machine tracking, backtest results assume zero execution latency and ignore order rejections, causing systematic overestimation of returns when actual fills are delayed or rejected in live trading

core.action: Use 14-period SMA of True Range for ATR calculation following Wilder's specification; do not substitute EMA, different window sizes, or alternative smoothing methods without explicit rationale

core.when: When implementing factor computation for high-dimensional portfolios (>50 assets)
core.action: Assume the framework implements covariance shrinkage (Ledoit-Wolf) — the framework uses sample correlation without shrinkage, leading to unstable covariance estimates in high-dimensional portfolios

consequence.description: Unstable covariance matrices produce unreliable portfolio weights, causing suboptimal diversification and significant losses when high-dimensional portfolios experience correlated drawdowns

core.when: When configuring portfolio optimization for high-dimensional portfolios
core.action: Implement or configure Ledoit-Wolf covariance shrinkage — apply oracle approximating shrinkage (OAS) or analytical shrinkage to stabilize the covariance matrix when estimating correlations across more than 50 assets

consequence.description: Without shrinkage, sample covariance becomes singular or near-singular in high dimensions, causing numerical instability and portfolio weights that are hypersensitive to small correlation changes

core.when: When implementing long-short strategy backtesting with leveraged positions
core.action: Assume the framework models funding costs for leveraged positions — the framework does not implement carry/funding cost modeling, causing long-short strategies to show inflated returns that cannot be replicated in live trading

consequence.description: Without funding cost modeling, long-short strategies appear more profitable in backtests than in live trading; the spread between borrowing costs and lending returns is ignored, leading to overestimated Sharpe ratios by 20-40%

core.action: Manually subtract funding costs from strategy returns — apply an estimated borrowing rate (e.g., 1-3% above risk-free rate) on net short exposure and lending rate on net long exposure to produce realistic net returns

consequence.description: Inflated backtest returns without funding costs lead to strategies that appear profitable but lose money in live trading after accounting for the cost of borrowing margin positions
